kimi-copilot.walkthrough.title: Kimi Models for GitHub Copilot Chat
kimi-copilot.walkthrough.description: Set up Kimi (Moonshot AI) models in GitHub Copilot Chat.
kimi-copilot.walkthrough.setApiKey.title: Set your Kimi API key
kimi-copilot.walkthrough.setApiKey.descr:
**Membership mode**: run `Kimi: Set API Key` to start the OAuth device-code login flow.
**Standard API mode**: [Get an API key](command:kimi-copilot.getApiKey) from platform.kimi.ai or platform.kimi.com.
[Set API Key](command:kimi-copilot.setApiKey)
kimi-copilot.walkthrough.chooseApi.title: Choose your Kimi API mode
kimi-copilot.walkthrough.chooseApi.descr:
Select between **Kimi Code Membership** (OAuth login, shared quota) and **Standard API** (pay-as-you-go, API key). For Standard API, also pick the matching region.
[Open settings](command:kimi-copilot.openSettings)
kimi-copilot.walkthrough.showModels.titl: Show Kimi models in the picker
kimi-copilot.walkthrough.showModels.desc:
Your Kimi models appear in the Copilot Chat model picker, filtered by your selected API Mode. If they are hidden, open the Language Models manager to show them.
[Open Language Models](command:workbench.action.chat.manage)
kimi-copilot.config.title: Kimi Models for GitHub Copilot Chat
kimi-copilot.config.apiMode.description:
Which Kimi API to use.

- **Kimi Code Membership** — uses your Kimi membership subscription via OAuth device-code login. Shared quota with Kimi会员. Best for coding with a subscription.
- **Standard API** — pay-as-you-go access via the Kimi Open Platform. The endpoint depends on the `region` setting.
kimi-copilot.config.apiMode.membership.d: Use your Kimi membership subscription via OAuth device-code login. Shared quota with Kimi会员. Endpoint: `api.kimi.com/coding/v1`.
kimi-copilot.config.apiMode.standard.des: Pay-as-you-go access via the Kimi Open Platform. The endpoint is chosen by the `region` setting.
kimi-copilot.config.region.description:
Server region for the **Standard API** mode (ignored when `baseUrl` is set or `apiMode` is `membership`). Keys from `platform.kimi.ai` and `platform.kimi.com` are independent — pick the region that matches where your key was created.

- **International** (`platform.kimi.ai`) — `api.moonshot.ai/v1`
- **Mainland China** (`platform.kimi.com`) — `api.moonshot.cn/v1`
kimi-copilot.config.region.china.label: Mainland China (platform.kimi.com)
kimi-copilot.config.baseUrl.description: Override the Kimi API base URL. When set, this value is used verbatim and `region` is ignored. Use this for self-hosted proxies or compatible OpenAI-style endpoints.
kimi-copilot.config.maxTokens.descriptio: Maximum number of output tokens per request. Set to `0` to use the API default (no explicit limit). Useful for controlling costs.
kimi-copilot.config.maxRetries.descripti: Maximum automatic retries for transient chat failures (HTTP 429 rate limits and 5xx server errors), not counting the initial attempt. Set to `0` to disable automatic retries and fail fast. Retries wait with exponential backoff and honor the server's `Retry-After` header.
kimi-copilot.config.modelIdOverrides.des: Remap a built-in model's API id. Keys are the model id shown in the picker (e.g. `kimi-k3`); values are the id sent to the API. Use this for proxies or endpoints that use different model names. To add brand-new models, use `customModels` instead.
kimi-copilot.config.customModels.descrip: Add your own Kimi models to the picker. Each entry is either a model id string (e.g. `"kimi-k3"`) or an object such as `{ "id": "my-model", "name": "My Model", "maxInputTokens": 262144 }`. Custom models are sent to the active endpoint.
kimi-copilot.config.thinking.description:
Whether Kimi uses step-by-step reasoning before answering. Applies to models with a toggleable thinking mode (`kimi-k2.6`, `kimi-k2.5`). `kimi-k3`, `kimi-k2.7-code`, and `kimi-for-coding` always reason.

- **On** — higher quality on complex coding and agent tasks (slower).
- **Off** — faster responses for simple edits.
kimi-copilot.config.debugLogging.descrip: Enable verbose debug logging to the Kimi output channel. Use [`Kimi: Show Logs`](command:kimi-copilot.showLogs) to view the channel. Logs may include request metadata but not prompt text.
kimi-copilot.config.usageRefreshInterval: How often (in minutes) to refresh the Kimi usage status bar. Minimum 1, maximum 1440 (24 hours). Shows membership quota (weekly/5h) or Standard API balance. Only available when no `baseUrl` override is configured.
kimi-copilot.config.showUsageStatusBar.d: Show the Kimi usage status-bar item. Displays membership quota (weekly/5h) or Standard API balance. Only available when no `baseUrl` override is configured.
